Off Calendar Jul-22-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint By Defendants Zimride, Inc And Lyft, Inc
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS LYFT, INC., AND ZIMRIDE, INC.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint does not adequately plead facts establishing that Defendants acted with oppression, fraud, or malice, as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294. Plaintiff cites Grimshaw v. Ford Motor Co. (1981) 119 Cal.App.3d 757, for the proposition that punitive damages are available in product liability cases. However, in Grimshaw, the Court found punitive damages were appropriate because Defendant Ford Motor Co.'s management had actual knowledge of the dangerous condition, but failed to take any remedial action. (Id. at 815.) Here, in contrast, there are no allegations that Defendants' management knew or should have known that the application was dangerous. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if he can in good faith, that Defendants knew or should have known that an application designed to be used while driving presents a dangerous condition because it necessarily requires a driver to look away from the road. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
